programmes.d: Cindy Hall and her husband Jamin Halberstadt moved from the US to Dunedin in the 1990s. For their build, Cindy is influenced by the Mountain Modern architectural style shed seen in houses surrounding Lake Tahoe in Nevada.

Sophie Thomson goes behind the scenes at a rose farm, Jane Edmanson rejuvenates potted plants, Costa Georgiadis visits a plant loving couple and we meet a landscape architect behind some of Australias most iconic gardens.

Emma Rebellato and Olivia Caisley are joined by Luke Siddham Dundon, Johnson Raela and Claudia Long to bring you the days news, sport and weather. Nate Byrne reports on Queensland floods, plus TC Hayley and NYE preparations.

programmes.t: A Symphonic Odyssey With Professor Brian Cox
programmes.d: Explore the secrets of the universe with Professor Brian Cox in this special event that combines ground-breaking science with the power of the Sydney Symphony Orchestra.
Host Anthony Burke meets a couple who have purchased one of the most significant buildings in the historic town of Kyneton, Victoria.
Bridget and Graeme Clarkes to-do list is long and complicated: theyre selling a house, repairing a house and building a house all at the same time, within a very hopeful 12 month period.
Bluey promises her customer Bingo the best tour ever in a game of Whale Watching, but they need Mum and Dad to join in and they are too tired to play after a late night.

programmes.t: New Years Eve 2025: Countdown to Midnight
programmes.d: Join in the celebration as as we count down to midnight with music from Melanie C, Matt Corby, The Cat Empire, Peach PRC, Marcia Hines, Casey Donovan, Don West and more. Hosted by Charlie Pickering and Zan Rowe.

programmes.t: Back In Time For The Corner Shop
programmes.d: In the 1970s, the Ferrones humble cornerstore has transformed to be more like todays mixed business convenience store. The hours are long and competition is fierce.

Nigella introduces us to her favourite recipes - whether its a tray of nourishing breakfast bars; a supper of crunchy chicken cutlets; or a sumptuous dark chocolate cake - every recipe is a guaranteed crowd pleaser.

Rob Brydon is back in the hosts chair, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as team captains. Sorting the fact from the fiction are guests Munya Chawawa, Bob Mortimer, Professor Alice Roberts and Snoochie Shy.
Nom Nom must successfully complete anger management.
When the box goes missing at a haunted mansion, the Bears play detective to help uncover the thief.
Gwen learns Clarissa has been keeping a big secret after she falls ill during a lacrosse game. Meanwhile, Irene hears that her father has a new love interest.
ABC Kids programs resume at 4.00am

When Shauns old friend the Alien drops by for a loo break, Shaun is only too happy to let him use the facilities in the farmhouse. While hes going about his business he leaves Shaun in charge of his intriguing new gadget.
Lil Nelson takes a job as a paperboy, which is more difficult than it sounds in a city overrun by scorching Pyro Vipers and an evil Serpentine sorceress...

The Bears find a bedazzled jean jacket in a dumpster. Its just about the coolest thing theyve ever seen and seems to make the wearer extremely lucky. But after the Bears fight over who gets control of the jacket.
The Doctors reveal what makes you flexible as they test the incredible strength of a tendon to the max, and they find out what its like to be Air Ambulance pilots in Operation Takeover.
The Bears help raise money to save Darrells store.
The classroom descends into chaos when June gets her hands on itching powder, but the fun ends when Connie becomes the victim of mean tricks.
ABC Kids programs resume at 4.00am

Its Timmys birthday and the Flock have thrown a party for him. At first hes delighted by all the attention; but when the Flock become distracted and hes left with no-one to play with, he decides to entertain himself.
The Serpentine attack on Ninjago City is seen from an unexpected perspective - entirely in the style of a live newscast delivered by Gayle Gossip and her cameraman.

Our teenage pups are negotiating their tricky adolescent months. Can troubled teen Rory avoid being rejected by yet another owner? Big Ron faces bath time; and giant pup Baloo cools down with a trip to the snow dome.

A rugged San Francisco police lieutenant, often criticised for his brutal handling of criminals, is turned loose on a psychotic sniper who is terrorising the city with threats of further daily killings.
Feeling the pressure of his parents, Lee makes an astonishing decision.
Julie joins the Art Club in order to befriend a cool art kid, but ends up being challenged to make her art more personal.
Robin convinces the Titans to go back to school with him.

programmes.t: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programmes.d: With her School of Friendship closed by the EEA, Twilight Sparkle must reunite her students, inspire her friends, and buck the rules to stand up for what she knows is right
RADI8 struggles to secure approval to debut after a member gets cut and Hannah teams up with an unlikely partner to get Sandra out.
Steven befriends a magical mirror that can mysteriously communicate with only him and ends up setting free its trapped inhabitant, a Gem named Lapis Lazuli.
Mythbusters have been putting out the call for myths to bust. And now its time to put a new batch of viewer suggestions to the test.

Phil discovers a house in Scotland that hasnt found a buyer in three years! Vivians moved to Ireland but Phil thinks if she spends a week bringing some life back to her former home, hell be able to get her a sale. (Final)
When a woman reports a murder and is then found strangled, the team are left wondering whether she was reporting her own murder.
rage soundtracks your countdown to the New Year with a party playlist guaranteed to get you moving.

Whilst Ron needs to please an important investor, Graham needs to please his wife Christine, who is desperate for a night out. At school, Alan is struggling to hide from the bullies that his dad is Graham Carr.
Ozzy accompanies Richelle to the pro-division qualifier, where she will face off against the best of the best. Meanwhile, Jude goes to great lengths to feel seen by Kenzie.
The Titans start taking their cues from fortune cookies.

programmes.t: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programmes.d: Pinkie Pies super-best-friend-sister bond is challenged when Maud gets a boyfriend that Pinkie cant stand.

In the early 80s, every boy racer wanted the Ford Escort RS1600i. But being driven hard and notoriously easy to steal, theres now only thought to be about 250 left in the U.K. Tim and Fuzz take on a sporty restoration.
The Jedi respond to a distress call.
Naughty Gaston is sent to pet school, where Miss Jolly soon gets him into shape.

A mans phone keeps ringing in a restaurant so he puts it in his briefcase. The Twirlywoos pass him the phone every time it rings. Going in and out of the briefcase until BigHoo throws the phone out and it lands in a glass.

Daniel is upset when he finds out he has a new babysitter who does things a little differently than Prince Tuesday. When he gives it some time, he changes his mind and has a tigertastic day exploring Jungle Beach with Amira.
Rain keeps the Teddies and Bananas indoors, so they play a game of hide and seek.
It is Granny and Grandpas anniversary. Peppa and George go to Mr Foxs Shop to find them a present. Mr Foxs Shop sells everything.

The kids decide to form a rock band with Paddington, but what instrument should he play and what is rock music? Its too loud to practice in the house, so where can they play? Will they break up before their park performance?
Peppa and her family spend the day jumping in different types of puddles but one type is still the best puddle in the world.
Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes help remove a thorn from the foot of a giant Asparagusaur who shows their gratitude by following them... everywhere.
Big Lizards found an epic crystal and Cosima is jealous. Their squabbling peaks at the top of the volcano! Will they learn to share before its too late?

programmes.t: Play School: Down At The Beach
programmes.d: Eddie and Teo pack the Beach Buggy with games for a day at the beach enjoying the sea and sand. They go on a shell hunt and tell the story of the Runaway Beach Ball.
The Mixmups go on an adventure to find out what the clouds taste like. When they cant reach up to the sky, will they find another way to discover how they taste?
Bing and Flop are finally ready to leave for their picnic just as it starts to rain. Bing is disappointed but as they unpack the wheelie bag he realises that they can have their picnic indoors
Bluey and Bingo would rather explore their holiday hotel room than relax on the beach.
Its sing-a-long time with Duggee and the Squirrels! Sing and dance with these original songs from Hey Duggee.
Join Captain Feathersword and The Wiggles for an exciting pirate dance adventure! After that, the fun continues its time to put on your tap shoes and get ready for the Wiggly World of Dance.

programmes.t: Thomas And Friends: All Engines Go!
programmes.d: When none of the engines want to delivery a super stinky package, they comically pass it back and forth, making a game out of it, until it spills and they end up with a big, smelly mess!
Pola is pretending to be a horse for Bobby and Tish but just before its Tishs turn, Pola gets up saying he wants a rest.
When Ayanas new adopted dog is an adorable chaos maker, the brothers find a way to not only drain some of that energy, but also train the little guy to be just as polite as Moose.
The Octonauts travel up a treacherous river to help a young migrating eel on her difficult journey.
Duggee and the Squirrels hop inside the Biology Bus and miniaturize themselves to go on a voyage of discovery inside Enid, to find out whats making her cough.
When the PA system blows out and sparks a fire at the Pontypandy Fun Run, its all hands-on deck to stop the spreading flames!

programmes.t: Play School: Big Teds Time Machine
programmes.d: Get ready to groove into the 1970s with Miah and Rachael. They lace up their roller-skates for a disco party, play music on vinyl and make tie-dye shirts! Special guest Georgie Parker helps tell the story of Sven at Ten.
Join Fizzy and Suds centre stage with ballerinas in beautiful tutus, take a peak backstage at an inspiring ballet performance and learn about pirouettes and pointe shoes!
Ginger teaches the baby Pea-Rexes a new survival skill through a game of hide and seek but things get out of hand when Split goes missing!
Mummy Pig shows Peppa how to pretend by teaching her how to wink. Peppa teaches George and Zoe Zebra, who has come to visit with her toy monkey.
Things get out of hand when Nevilles plan for a peaceful day of floating on the river turns into a wild race.
Newton, An Yu and the PJ Masks become prisoners of the team of supercharged villains - Wholl save them?
Windsor Gardens has two new heroes - Mr Brilliant and Rare Bear, but will Jonathan and Judy be impressed?

A mole disturbs King Thistles Golf Course, so a Gnome is enlisted to help drive it away. Soon though the Gnome is ordering the Elves around, and turning the Kings Golf Course into a Crazy Golf Course.
Buff trains with Batwing to improve his gracefulness.
The younglings investigate odd noises in the junkyard.
With so many rescue missions, Elf Rescue needs a helping hand from Children Rescue.

The man is packing his suitcase ready for a holiday. He stretches his goggles and swimming trunks before placing them in the suitcase. The Twirlywoos have a go at stretching too, what will the man wear to go swimming in now?
Daniel and O the Owl are at Katerinas for a sleepover. O realizes its going to be a little different than how he usually sleeps he wants to go home.
The Bananas and Lulu are making a see-saw in the park, but they cant get it to balance.
Peppa and George notice they have shadows, and no matter how fast they walk, run, or jump - they cant lose them.
After a neighbourhood watch talk, Paddington decides to go on patrol as an unofficial policeman. Soon, he has to solve a real crime, but who is the litterbug?
Peppas Playgroup have a new friend to meet - Mandy Mouse. Mandy uses a wheelchair. The children have a lot of fun playing with Mandy as she is super sporty and loves to play games.
Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes have to tread ever so lightly if they want to retrieve their ball from a cluster of sleeping Cucumberdiles - but keeping a low profile is not in a Pea-Rexes nature.
A dragonfly steals a bee cocoon that Cosima had vowed to keep safe. Cosima and Big Lizard give chase, leading to a surprising solution with a vacuum cleaner and some sticky vines!

programmes.t: Play School: Down At The Beach
programmes.d: There are lots of jobs to do at the beach! Emma and Eddie are joined by special guest Surf Life Savers, Chelsea, Courtney and helpful Nipper Big Ted. They tell the story of Sallys Sunhat Saga and discover jobs at the beach.

The Mixmups go on a nighttime adventure to try and stay awake more than the moon, but the harder they try, the sleepier they become. Can they find a way to stay up until morning and become wide awake winners?
Bing and Sula find a helium balloon stuck in a tree in the park. They both want to play with it at the same time so Flop makes two strings and they invent a noisy balloon game called Stuck Duckies.
On a trip to the beach, Mum teaches Bluey to throw, while Bingo and Dad get creative with a funny shaped stick.
Its sing-a-long time with Duggee and the Squirrels! Sing and dance with these original songs from Hey Duggee.
Count along to One, Two, Three, Four, Five, and join the fun! Paloma the Mermaid is visiting today but Lucia, Simon, and Lachy have lost their Wiggle. Can Tsehay help them find it before she arrives?
Mummy Pig shows Peppa how to pretend by teaching her how to wink. Peppa teaches George and Zoe Zebra, who has come to visit with her toy monkey.
Tish and Bobby are stuck outside on an extremely hot day. Determined not to be without a house, Tish walks around the garden looking for a solution.
The Dream Factory Crew build a Basketball Helper Machine to help them perfect their shooting skills for the Steamport sports camp.
Down in the darkness of the ocean deep, Peso and the Octonauts must help a tiny, injured Dwarf Lanternshark. But they have to find him first!

Happy sets his sights on a very questionable looking puddle that he is determined to splash in, much to the horror of Duggee and the other Squirrels - and the tiny citizens of Midgetropolis who live inside it!
A quest to find the Ancient Pontypandy Way turns into a twilight forest fire rescue, and the Firefighters get to test out their new uniforms!

programmes.t: Play School: Big Teds Time Machine
programmes.d: Emma and Teo turn back the clock to the radical 1980s! Put in the cassette and slide on your leg warmers for an aerobics session with Shannon Dooley! Big Ted gets into 80s games and George Spartels joins in too.
Fizzy and Suds take us for a ride on a giant radio telescope and discover the Emu Constellation. Lets learn about space together!
Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes discover a new game - Pea-Rex Pinball! Its all fun and games until they suddenly find themselves cornered by a horde of super irritated Vegesaurs.

Peppa and George are running and playing in the garden. Rebecca Rabbit joins them and shows them how to hop, then Suzy Sheep appears and shows them how to skip and Pedro shows up and challenges them to Hop Skip and Jump!
When Pounce competes against Reef and Roadie in a jumping contest, his friends worry hes putting himself in danger.
An enchanting story about two devoted scarecrows, who are planning a wedding to remember. Will they be able to overcome disaster and celebrate the wedding that no-one will ever forget?
Badcomputer captures Bibi and disguises Jestah to take her place.

Cindy Hall and her husband Jamin Halberstadt moved from the US to Dunedin in the 1990s. For their build, Cindy is influenced by the Mountain Modern architectural style shed seen in houses surrounding Lake Tahoe in Nevada.

Sophie Thomson goes behind the scenes at a rose farm, Jane Edmanson rejuvenates potted plants, Costa Georgiadis visits a plant loving couple and we meet a landscape architect behind some of Australias most iconic gardens.

Emma Rebellato and Olivia Caisley are joined by Luke Siddham Dundon, Johnson Raela and Claudia Long to bring you the days news, sport and weather. Nate Byrne reports on Queensland floods, plus TC Hayley and NYE preparations.

programmes.t: A Symphonic Odyssey With Professor Brian Cox
programmes.d: Explore the secrets of the universe with Professor Brian Cox in this special event that combines ground-breaking science with the power of the Sydney Symphony Orchestra.
Host Anthony Burke meets a couple who have purchased one of the most significant buildings in the historic town of Kyneton, Victoria.
Bridget and Graeme Clarkes to-do list is long and complicated: theyre selling a house, repairing a house and building a house all at the same time, within a very hopeful 12 month period.
Bluey promises her customer Bingo the best tour ever in a game of Whale Watching, but they need Mum and Dad to join in and they are too tired to play after a late night.

programmes.t: New Years Eve 2025: Countdown to Midnight
programmes.d: Join in the celebration as as we count down to midnight with music from Melanie C, Matt Corby, The Cat Empire, Peach PRC, Marcia Hines, Casey Donovan, Don West and more. Hosted by Charlie Pickering and Zan Rowe.

programmes.t: Back In Time For The Corner Shop
programmes.d: In the 1970s, the Ferrones humble cornerstore has transformed to be more like todays mixed business convenience store. The hours are long and competition is fierce.

Nigella introduces us to her favourite recipes - whether its a tray of nourishing breakfast bars; a supper of crunchy chicken cutlets; or a sumptuous dark chocolate cake - every recipe is a guaranteed crowd pleaser.

Rob Brydon is back in the hosts chair, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as team captains. Sorting the fact from the fiction are guests Munya Chawawa, Bob Mortimer, Professor Alice Roberts and Snoochie Shy.
Solua Middleton takes you on a journey through some of this countrys ancient and epic past, exploring Indigenous Australias deep history with stories passed down through countless generations.

The latest international news and the days top stories from a European perspective. Our broadcast partner DW brings you live coverage from Berlin with updated reports and expert analysis on the big global issues and events.

A wellness revolution has captured the White House. Donald Trumps ex-wife Marla Maples reveals the inside story behind the Make America Healthy Again movement and the role she played in uniting Robert F Kennedy Jr and Trump.
Pioneers dont just belong in centuries past. A dairy farming family in Queenslands Sunshine Coast hinterland is pioneering the farming of water buffalo to produce milk and meat.

Jim Maxwell bowls an over of memories with his six favourite Ashes Moments. The final one comes from 2005 at Edgbaston, as England claimed an astonishing victory that spawned the iconic Brett Lee/Andrew Flintoff photo.
We continue our look back at more of our favourite Landline stories from the past year. Tasting panels helping to breed better mangoes; plus using basalt to fertilise farms and capture carbon.

Emma Rebellato and Olivia Caisley are joined by Luke Siddham Dundon, Johnson Raela and Claudia Long to bring you the days news, sport and weather. Nate Byrne reports on Queensland floods, plus TC Hayley and NYE preparations.

programmes.t: Y2k: The Bug That Didnt Bite
programmes.d: On New Years Eve in 1999, the world held its breath when the date ticked over to 1/1/2000. John Barron looks at how Australia beat the millennium bug, and whether global panic over a Y2K computer meltdown was warranted.

Ruby Cornish brings you the days top stories and latest news including flooding across North Queensland, severe winds and heavy rain after Tropical Cyclone Hayley hit north-west WA, plus New Years Eve preparations and more.

Indira Naidoo in conversation with Stan Grant, exploring his deepening faith, Indigenous spirituality, his response to the Voice referendum, and the pivotal relationship with his father, a Wiradjuri elder.

Joe OBrien brings you the days top stories and latest news including flooding across North Queensland, severe winds and heavy rain after Tropical Cyclone Hayley hit north-west WA, plus New Years Eve preparations and more.

Joe OBrien presents the latest from ABC News, following todays top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport and weather you need on New Years Eve.

The latest breaking news and a comprehensive roundup of todays best stories from across Australias states and regions. With reporters in more places ABC News tells the stories that matter to you. Presented by Manny Tsigas.

Manny Tsigas presents the latest from ABC News, following todays top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport and weather you need across the summer holidays.
The ABCs network of international correspondents take us back through the stories that helped shape The World in 2025. Presented by Girish Sawlani.
Live coverage of the world-famous midnight fireworks display lighting up Sydneys Harbour Bridge, Opera House and surrounds, as Australia bids a colourful farewell to 2025 and welcome in 2026.

The latest international news and the days top stories from a European perspective. Our broadcast partner DW brings you live coverage from Berlin with updated reports and expert analysis on the big global issues and events.

The latest international news and the days top stories from a European perspective. Our broadcast partner DW brings you live coverage from Berlin with updated reports and expert analysis on the big global issues and events.

The former Soviet republic of Georgia is fighting for its very existence as a democracy. With dissenters being jailed and a raft of draconian laws passed, opposition figures claim neighbouring Russia is pulling the strings.
Australia is in the midst of a cannabis boom. Not black-market marijuana, but pharmaceutical grade medicinal cannabis.

Julia Baird is joined in conversation with all five 2025 Boyer lecturers, Justin Wolfers, John Anderson, Larissa Behrendt, Amelia Lester and James Curran to reflect on the theme Australia: A Radical Experiment in Democracy.
More of our favourite Landline stories from the past year. Australias falling farm productivity rate, and celebrating the Italian heritage of Victorias King Valley wines.

Start 2026 with the latest news, the days top stories and a look at New Years Eve festivities. Emma Rebellato and Olivia Caisley bring you news and interviews, joined by Luke Siddham Dundon, Johnson Raela and Claudia Long.

Back Roads explores Western Australias beloved Bibbulmun Track - a 1000 kilometre walk through mighty forests and a rugged coastline. Guest Presenter Rae Johnston meets people transformed by the experience.
How an online love scam landed West Australian grandmother Donna Nelson in solitary confinement in Japan, leaving her five daughters fighting for her freedom.

On the Mexican border 10,000 troops have been deployed to stop the traffic of the deadly drug fentanyl. The show of force by Mexico follows the threat of crippling US tariffs, but can the crackdown stop the drug cartels?

programmes.t: David Brills Story On The Frontlines
programmes.d: A life through the lens - the story of Legendary ABC cinematographer David Brill looks back at his incredible career on the frontlines of history, from the 1960s and Vietnam to Four Corners and beyond.
